name: The Safeguard Group - Housing & Debt Advisors
description_short: Non-profit housing and debt counselor offering HUD-compliant mortgage, financial, and homebuyer education services via phone counseling in New York.
description_long:
The Safeguard Group - Housing & Debt Advisors is a non-profit credit counseling organization based in Northport, New York, providing housing and financial guidance to consumers navigating debt and homeownership challenges. The organization specializes in mortgage-related counseling and operates as part of the broader affordable housing assistance ecosystem, listed on PublicAffordableHousing.com as an approved counselor. Their service delivery model emphasizes accessibility through phone-based counseling, allowing clients to receive guidance remotely without requiring in-person visits. The Safeguard Group positions itself as a comprehensive advisor addressing both housing stability and debt management simultaneously, recognizing the interconnection between mortgage health and overall financial wellness.

The organization offers a range of HUD-standard counseling services including pre-purchase homebuyer education, mortgage delinquency resolution, financial management and budgeting support, and post-purchase workshops. Services span the homeownership lifecycle from prospective buyers seeking education before purchase through existing homeowners facing mortgage default or seeking to prevent delinquency. They provide individual counseling sessions as well as group workshops covering financial, budgeting, and credit topics. All services are conducted in English via phone counseling at 1-800-673-6993, with email contact available through rcornejo@safeguardcredit.org.

The Safeguard Group's primary distinction is its integrated focus on housing counseling combined with debt advisory services, rather than offering credit repair or debt settlement. As a free-help resource, they operate under non-profit structure without requiring fees for counseling services, aligned with HUD standards for housing counselors. Their phone-based counseling model removes geographic barriers for New York residents and potentially broader service areas. The combination of pre-purchase education and delinquency resolution services addresses both preventive and crisis-intervention needs.

The primary limitation is the lack of publicly available detailed information about certifications, outcomes, or specific counselor credentials beyond the organization's listing as an approved housing counselor. No data is provided regarding average client outcomes, wait times, or success rates in mortgage delinquency resolution. The organization's website presence appears minimal, with the 404 error on their "About" page limiting transparency. Prospective clients must contact the organization directly to verify current services, specializations, or any evolving service offerings.
pros: Phone-based counseling removes geographic barriers for accessibility across service regions
Comprehensive pre-purchase homebuyer education workshops help prevent future default
Mortgage delinquency and default resolution counseling serves homeowners in crisis
Combined housing and debt advisory approach addresses interconnected financial challenges
Free counseling services with no upfront fees, removing cost barriers to assistance
HUD-approved counselor status indicates compliance with federal housing counseling standards
Financial management and budgeting workshops provide preventive education beyond crisis intervention
cons: Minimal online presence and 404 errors on their website limit transparency about organization details
No publicly available information about counselor certifications, experience levels, or staff credentials
No outcome data, success metrics, or client testimonials provided to verify effectiveness
English-only services limit accessibility for non-English speakers in diverse New York communities
No information about wait times, appointment availability, or service capacity constraints
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking pre-purchase education and guidance in New York
Homeowners facing mortgage delinquency or default who need crisis intervention counseling
Consumers seeking free financial management and budgeting education without commercial sales pressure
diagnosis: The Safeguard Group is best for New York homebuyers and homeowners seeking free, HUD-compliant housing counseling without commercial pressure. The primary caveat is that limited online transparency requires direct contact to verify current services, counselor qualifications, and service availability, and no publicly available outcome data exists to assess effectiveness.
services: Mortgage Delinquency and Default Resolution Counseling
Resolving and Preventing Mortgage Delinquency Workshops
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
